title: lens orientalis
intro: Lens orientalis, also known as Indian gentian or gentian, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Asia. It possesses medicinal properties due to its high concentration of bitter compounds, such as gentianine and amarogentin, which have been traditionally used to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and nausea. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-diabetic properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and manage blood sugar levels. Lens orientalis can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tea, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders.
